item.text: Both local commands passed: the focused regression ran 1 test successfully, and the full `cf-modkit-odata` library suite ran 95 tests successfully. I’m submitting the review artifact with no blockers.

arguments.summary_title: OData single-character identifier regression covered and passing
arguments.summary_markdown:
Reviewed the applied implementation for bug6. The parser change in `libs/modkit-odata/src/odata_parse.rs` changes only the identifier suffix repetition from one-or-more to zero-or-more, preserving the required first-character rule while allowing one-character identifiers.

Test coverage was added in `libs/modkit-odata/src/odata_parse_tests.rs` via `single_character_identifier`, which calls `parse_str("x eq 1")` and asserts the parsed expression is an equality comparison between identifier `x` and numeric value `1`.

Verification passed locally and matches the provided CI result: `cargo test -p cf-modkit-odata --lib single_character_identifier` passed with 1 test, and `cargo test -p cf-modkit-odata --lib` passed with 95 tests, 0 failures, and 0 ignored tests. No regressions or review blockers were found.

tests_run.log: cargo test -p cf-modkit-odata --lib single_character_identifier: 1 passed; 0 failed; 94 filtered out. cargo test -p cf-modkit-odata --lib: 95 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored.
